schools.summary: Gill: an exact account of these inheritances is given in the following chapters.
Henry: the historian now turns to the disposal of the land west of Jordan.

themes: these are the countries which the children of Israel inherited... Eleazar the priest, and Joshua... distributed
the inheritance ordered by lawful authority and counsel
let all things be done decently and in order (1 Cor. 14:40)

schools.summary: Gill: the lot was used to prevent quarrels and strife, the disposing being of the Lord, and to show the agreement between the lot and the divine purpose.

themes: By lot was their inheritance, as the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses
God himself appointing each their portion by lot
the whole disposing thereof is of the LORD (Prov. 16:33)

schools.summary: Gill: the two tribes of Gad and Reuben, and the half tribe of Manasseh, already described; to the Levites was given no inheritance among them.

themes: Moses had given the inheritance of two tribes and an half... but unto the Levites he gave none inheritance
God providing differently, the LORD himself Levi's portion
I am thy part and thine inheritance (Num. 18:20)

schools.summary: Gill: the birthright forfeited by Reuben given to Joseph, who had the double portion, keeping the number of twelve; the Levites had cities with suburbs.

themes: the children of Joseph were two tribes... they gave no part unto the Levites... save cities to dwell in
God ordering the inheritance and dispersing his ministers
the priest's lips should keep knowledge (Mal. 2:7)

schools.summary: Gill: they did this by their heads and representatives, the high priest, chief ruler, and princes of the tribes.
Clarke: the obedience to the LORD's command.

themes: As the LORD commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did, and they divided the land
ordering all by God's word, not their own devising
this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments (1 John 5:3)

schools.summary: Gill: these were some chief men of Judah accompanying Caleb for his honour, he being their prince; Caleb recalled what the LORD had said of him and Joshua at Kadesh-barnea.

themes: Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite said... Thou knowest the thing that the LORD said... in Kadeshbarnea
pleading not merit but the faithfulness of God's word
hath he said, and shall he not do it? (Num. 23:19)

schools.summary: Gill: he and eleven others were sent, one from each tribe, of like age, to show they were men of years and judgment; he brought word again as it was in his heart.

themes: Forty years old was I when Moses... sent me... to espy out the land; and I brought him word again as it was in mine heart
faithfulness speaking truth against the unbelief of many
a true witness delivereth souls (Prov. 14:25)

schools.summary: Gill: the rest of the spies, excepting Joshua, who went up with him into the land.
Wesley: Caleb wholly following the LORD.

themes: my brethren... made the heart of the people melt: but I wholly followed the LORD my God
true faith following the LORD with the whole heart
the LORD is with you, while ye be with him (2 Chr. 15:2)

schools.summary: Gill: it was the Lord that sware to what follows, Moses declaring the oath of the Lord.
Keil & Delitzsch: the promise that the land he trod should be his inheritance.

themes: the land whereon thy feet have trodden shall be thine inheritance... because thou hast wholly followed the LORD
God rewarding wholehearted devotion with a sure portion
be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee a crown of life (Rev. 2:10)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord had upheld him in life and health, preserving him from many dangers in the wilderness, according to his promise; he was now eighty-five.

themes: the LORD hath kept me alive, as he said, these forty and five years... I am this day fourscore and five years old
life and preservation owed to God's faithfulness
thou hast granted me life and favour, and... preserved my spirit (Job 10:12)

schools.summary: Gill: he was as sound in mind, judgment, and memory, and as robust in body, as forty-five years before — a wonderful instance; his strength was as much for war now as then.

themes: As yet I am as strong this day as I was in the day that Moses sent me... for war
God sustaining his faithful servant in fullness of power
they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength (Isa. 40:31)

schools.summary: Gill: he asked not one city only but a large tract, the hill country where Hebron and Debir were.
Clarke: his confidence that the LORD would be with him to drive out the Anakim.

themes: Now therefore give me this mountain... how the Anakims were there... if so be the LORD will be with me, then I shall be able to drive them out
bold faith seeking the hardest task, trusting God's presence
if God be for us, who can be against us? (Rom. 8:31)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua blessed him by granting his request, congratulating him, and wishing him success against the giants.
Keil & Delitzsch: Hebron given to Caleb.

themes: And Joshua blessed him, and gave unto Caleb the son of Jephunneh Hebron for an inheritance
faithfulness honoured and its request granted
no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly (Ps. 84:11)

schools.summary: Gill: 'unto this day' means to the time of the writing of the book.
Keil & Delitzsch: Hebron as Caleb's inheritance because he wholly followed the LORD.

themes: Hebron therefore became the inheritance of Caleb... because that he wholly followed the LORD God of Israel
wholehearted following bringing an enduring reward
he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever (1 John 2:17)

schools.summary: Gill: Kirjath-arba, 'the city of Arba,' named from the greatest man among the Anakim; the land had rest from war.

themes: the name of Hebron before was Kirjatharba; which Arba was a great man among the Anakims. And the land had rest from war
faithful following leading at last to rest
there remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God (Heb. 4:9)
